zh.lifeSummary: 當Bernhard II. Billung Herzog von Sachsen於0995年出生時，他的父親Bernhard I. Billung Herzog von Sachsen45歲，母親Hildegard von Stade25歲。他於1019年與Eilika von Schweinfurt結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於1059年6月29日在德國尼德薩辛盧包格享年64歲，埋葬在德國尼德薩辛盧包格。

nameMeanings.name: Billung
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: either a patronymic from the Middle English personal name Billing, derived from Old English bil ‘sword’, or a habitational name from Great and Little Billing in Northamptonshire, meaning ‘(settlement of) the followers (Old English -ingas) of a man called Bill(a) or Bȳdel’.
German: from an ancient Germanic personal name, formed with a cognate of Old Saxon bīl ‘sword’.
Danish and Norwegian: from an Old Danish personal name of uncertain origin.

variants.name: Billings
meanings: English (Midlands): variant of Billing , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Belling
meanings: German: habitational name from any of several places called Belling, in Pomerania, Baden, Hesse, Thuringia, and elsewhere.

English (Devon): perhaps from the Middle English personal name Belling, Beling, of uncertain origin (perhaps from Old English Bǣdling, an original nickname from bǣdling ‘effeminate man’). Alternatively, a variant of Billing .

variants.name: Billinger
meanings: German: from the personal name   Billing   2.

nameMeanings.name: Bernhard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Bernd
meanings: From an Old French name of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from ber(n) ‘bear’ + hard ‘hardy, brave, strong’. This was the name of three famous medieval churchmen: St Bernard of Menthon ( 923–1008 ), founder of a hospice on each of the Alpine passes named after him; the monastic reformer St Bernard of Clairvaux ( 1090–1153 ); and the scholastic philosopher Bernard of Chartres. It was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to England. A native Old English form, Beornheard, was superseded by the Norman form.

variants.name: Bernard
meanings: From an Old French name of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from ber(n) ‘bear’ + hard ‘hardy, brave, strong’. This was the name of three famous medieval churchmen: St Bernard of Menthon ( 923–1008 ), founder of a hospice on each of the Alpine passes named after him; the monastic reformer St Bernard of Clairvaux ( 1090–1153 ); and the scholastic philosopher Bernard of Chartres. It was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to England. A native Old English form, Beornheard, was superseded by the Norman form.

nameMeanings.name: II.
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Violet  ,   Vivien  , or   Vivian .

variants.name: Vivien
meanings: Earlier generally taken as a variant of the boy's name Vivian , but now also rarely used for boys. This spelling was quite common in Old French. Its use as a girl's name in the English-speaking world was influenced by Tennyson's Merlin and Vivien ( 1859 ). This name, from Arthurian legend, may represent an altered form of a Celtic name (perhaps akin to the Irish Gaelic name Béibhinn ‘white lady’, pronounced ‘bee-veen’). The actress Vivien Leigh ( 1913–67 ) was christened Vivian.

variants.name: Violet
meanings: From the name of the flower (Old French violette, Late Latin violetta, a diminutive of viola). This was one of the earliest flower names to become popular in Britain, being used as early as 1700 and becoming well established in the 19th century, although it is now somewhat out of favour.

variants.name: Vivian
meanings: Originally a boy's name, from an Old French form of the Latin name Vivianus (probably a derivative of vivus ‘alive’), but now more frequent as a girl's name. The name was borne by a 5th-century bishop of Saintes in western France, remembered for protecting his people during the invasion of the Visigoths.

en.lifeSummary: When Bernhard II. Billung Herzog von Sachsen was born in 0995, his father, Bernhard I. Billung Herzog von Sachsen, was 45 and his mother, Hildegard von Stade, was 25. He married Eilika von Schweinfurt in 1019. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 3 daughters. He died on 29 June 1059, in Lüneburg, Lower Saxony, Germany, at the age of 64, and was buried in Lüneburg, Lower Saxony, Germany.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Bernhard II. Billung Herzog von Sachsen est né en 0995, son père, Bernhard I. Billung Herzog von Sachsen, avait 45 ans et sa mère, Hildegard von Stade, avait 25 ans. Il a épousé Eilika von Schweinfurt en 1019. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 3 filles. Il est décédé le 29 juin 1059, à Lüneburg, Niedersachsen, Allemagne, à l’âge de 64 ans, et a été enterré à Lüneburg, Niedersachsen, Allemagne.

Er hat 1019 Eilika von Schweinfurt geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Er starb am 29. Juni 1059 im Alter von 64 Jahren in Lüneburg, Niedersachsen, Deutschland und wurde in Lüneburg, Niedersachsen, Deutschland begraben.

ko.lifeSummary: Bernhard II. Billung Herzog von Sachsen(이)가 0995년에 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Bernhard I. Billung Herzog von Sachsen(은)는 45세였고, 그의 어머니 Hildegard von Stade(은)는 25세였다. 그는 1019년 Eilika von Schweinfurt(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1059년 6월 29일 독일 니더작센 u니버그에서 64세의 나이로 사망했으며 독일 니더작센 u니버그에 묻혔다.

Ele casou-se com Eilika von Schweinfurt em 1019. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 3 filhas. ru.lifeSummary: Когда Bernhard II. Billung Herzog von Sachsen родился в 0995 г., его отцу по имени Bernhard I. Billung Herzog von Sachsen было 45 лет, а матери, которую звали Hildegard von Stade, 25. Он женился на женщине по имени Eilika von Schweinfurt в 1019 г. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 3 дочери. Он умер 29 июня 1059 г. в местности лuнебург, ниедирсачсен, Германия в возрасте 64 лет и был похоронен в местности лuнебург, ниедирсачсен, Германия.
